result:
Subject: Yesterday's payments outage — what happened and what we're doing

Yesterday, for roughly four hours, our payments service was degraded. During that window, some of you tried to complete transactions and saw them fail outright. If that was you, I'm sorry. Payments are the one thing that has to just work, and yesterday ours didn't.

The root cause was a misconfigured cache invalidation rule on our side. Under a traffic spike, the rule evicted entries faster than they could be repopulated, which starved downstream payment authorizations and caused the failures you experienced. This was our mistake — not your bank's, not your network's, and not anything you did differently.

The immediate fix is already live: we corrected the invalidation rule and added guardrails so it can't behave this way again under load. To prevent a repeat, we're moving all cache and payment-path changes behind canary rollouts, and we're adding chaos testing in staging to deliberately simulate traffic spikes and cache failures before changes ever reach production.

For anyone whose transaction failed during the incident, we're applying a goodwill credit to your account automatically — you don't need to do anything. If you had a failed payment and don't see the credit within 48 hours, reply to this email and we'll make it right. Thank you for your patience, and again, I'm sorry we let you down.

— The Payments Team
